{"detail":"ambiguous_scope: Three blockers prevent safe rewrite or claim. (1) Dependency fizeau-b2c5c826 ('bead A's ranker') does not exist in the codebase; its package path, exported function signatures, and input/output contract are unknown. The bead cannot be implemented without them, and inventing them would be out of intake scope. (2) The catalog AutoRoutable check against discovery model strings is underspecified: the cassette emits harness-local strings ('opus-4.7', 'gpt-5.5') while catalog.ModelEligibility() keyed by canonical ID or surface ID; the surface name to use (SurfaceCodex='codex' vs SurfaceClaudeCode='claude-code') for the claude runner is never stated, and the catalog currently has no surface mapping that would make 'opus-4.7' auto-routable via any surface called 'claude' or matching the claude CLI runner. The catalog entry claude-opus-4.7 only declares surfaces agent.anthropic and claude-code; whether the implementation should use SurfaceClaudeCode or a discovery-side string match is left implicit. (3) The claude acceptance criterion is hedged ('resolves to opus-4.7 or whatever the version-newest auto-routable model is given the cassette content'), which is not a pinnable regression test — the regression test the bead promises would need a fixed expected value. Additionally, scope item 1 describes background PTY goroutines for TTL refresh while the out-of-scope section says 'Live PTY refresh… use cassette default; refresh is operator-triggered via existing tooling' — a direct contradiction. All three issues require operator resolution before an implementable prompt can be produced.","intake_outcome":"operator_required"}
Operator rewrote the bead to name actual ranker APIs, catalog surfaces, fixed expected defaults, and no-live-refresh scope; retry now.
